protocol.name: xdg_toplevel_drag_v1
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: xdg_toplevel_drag_manager_v1

description.summary: Move a window during a drag

interface.description:
This protocol enhances normal drag and drop with the ability to move a window at the same time. This allows having detachable panels similar to what desktop environments done with subwindows.
A typical workflow would be: - The user starts a drag from a special "tab" which may be dragged out of its panel. - Once the user released the button, if the user dragged outside of the panel the "tab" becomes a toplevel window. - If the user dropped onto another tab, the panel handles the drop as it sees fit.
This protocol is not limited to this one use-case; a compositor could advertise the protocol even if it does not support this particular combination. However a compositor that does not support this would be limited to handling it as a regular drop.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the xdg_toplevel_drag_manager_v1 object. This does not affect any remaining objects created from this manager.

request.name: get_xdg_toplevel_drag

description.summary: get an xdg_toplevel_drag for a wl_data_source

request.description: Create an xdg_toplevel_drag for a drag and drop operation that is starting. This request can only be made on sources used in drag-and-drop, so it must be performed before wl_data_device.start_drag. Attempting to use the source after it has been used for a clipboard selection is a client error. The compositor may also require a minimum version of the data device manager to use this. Compositors should not consider xdg_toplevel_drag_manager_v1 to be supported if they do not support the minimum version.

arg.name: data_source
arg.summary: The data source used for the drag-and-drop operation

interface.name: xdg_toplevel_drag_v1

description.summary: Object representing a toplevel move during a drag

interface.description: An object that allows a toplevel to be moved during a drag-and-drop operation.

entry.name: invalid_source
entry.summary: data_source was already used for another toplevel drag

entry.name: toplevel_destroyed
entry.summary: the toplevel was destroyed before the drag ended

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroy this xdg_toplevel_drag object. This request must only be called after the data source object that was given to get_xdg_toplevel_drag has been destroyed or if the toplevel drag has already ended. Destroying this object while a drag is active does not affect the drag. Drag events should be sent to the wl_data_source object, and clients should handle drops through the wl_data_source cancelled/action_failed event.

request.name: attach

description.summary: Attach a toplevel to this drag

request.description:
Request that the compositor detaches the current toplevel and starts a drag-and-drop operation with the toplevel being dragged. The drag must have been started on the toplevel being the drag origin in wl_data_device.start_drag.
If the toplevel is destroyed before the drag ends, the compositor should treat this as the toplevel becoming the drag icon for the remainder of the drag. The offset describes where in the window the grab point is, allowing the compositor to position the window correctly in relation to the cursor. The offset is in local (surface) coordinate of the toplevel surface.
